1: And whosoever is deceived thereby is not wise.
2: Whoso Whoso provoketh him to anger sinneth against against his own soul.
3: But every fool will be meddling.
4: Therefore Therefore shall he beg in harvest, and have have nothing.
5: But a man of understanding will draw it out.
6: But a faithful man who can find?
7: His children are are blessed after him.
8: Scattereth away all evil with his eyes.
9: I am pure from my sin?
10: Both of them are are alike abomination to the LORD LORD .
11: Whether his work be be pure, and whether it be it be right.
12: The LORD LORD hath made even both of them.
13: Open thine eyes, and and thou shalt be satisfied with bread.
14: But when he is gone his way, then he boasteth.
15: But the lips of knowledge are are a precious jewel.
16: And take a pledge of him for a strange woman.
17: But afterwards his mouth shall be filled with gravel.
18: And with good advice make war.
19: Therefore meddle not with him that flattereth with his lips.
20: His lamp shall be put out in obscure darkness.
21: But the end thereof shall not be blessed.
22: But But wait on the LORD LORD , and he shall save thee.
23: And a false balance is is not good.
24: How can a man then understand his own way?
25: And after vows to make enquiry.
26: And bringeth the wheel over them.
27: Searching all the inward parts of the belly.
28: And his throne is upholden by mercy.
29: And the beauty of old men is is the grey head.
30: So do do stripes the inward parts of the belly.
